description.undefined: Ontology for modeling population health indicators based on health DCAT-AP data catalogue

comments.undefined: An ontology for describing population health indicators, created during the Older Adult Health and Wellbeing Profile project.

source.value: OAHP Data Catalogue Schema Proposal v05.13

abstract.value: The Population Health Indicator Ontology (phi) provides a formal vocabulary for describing population health indicators, their definitions, data sources, methodologies, and quality attributes. Developed as part of the Older Adult Health and Wellbeing Profile project. Funded by Health Service Executive National Clinical Programme for Older People Research Award 2024 (NCPOP RA3/2024)

rights.value: Copyright © 2025 Health Service Executive (HSE) and University College Dublin (UCD). This work is licensed under a Creative Commons Attribution 4.0 International License.

bibliographicCitation.value: Rob Brennan, Junli Liang and Akila Wickramasekara, , Prototyping a Health DCAT-AP data catalogue to support population health indicator identification and quality assessment, NeXt-generation Data Governance workshop 2025 (NXDG 2025), https://ceur-ws.org/Vol-4064/NXDG25-paper3.pdf

source.value: OAHP Data Catalogue Schema Proposal v05.13

comment.undefined: A population health indicator that measures specific aspects of health and wellbeing.

source.value: DCAT3

comment.undefined: Collection of data published or curated by a single agent and available for access or download in one or more serializations or formats

source.value: OAHP Data Catalogue Schema Proposal v05.13

comment.undefined: A grouping of related health profile indicators.

source.value: OAHP Data Catalogue Schema Proposal v05.13

comment.undefined: A summary explaining why this indicator matters—its relevance to population health, what it helps to understand, and how it complements other indicators.

source.value: OAHP Data Catalogue Schema Proposal v05.13

comment.undefined: Identifier, e.g. URL, for the dataset providing a denominator data element. If there are multiple denominator data elements then multiple datasets must be provided as a list.

source.value: OAHP Data Catalogue Schema Proposal v05.13

comment.undefined: Specifies if the indicator has both a numerator and a denominator, is coverage, accumulated or qualitative type

source.value: OAHP Data Catalogue Schema Proposal v05.13

comment.undefined: Full name of the indicator as used in reports or dashboards.

source.value: OAHP Data Catalogue Schema Proposal v05.13

comment.undefined: Summary of methods used to calculate the indicator (e.,g statistical technique, smoothing, standardisation, modelling).

source.value: OAHP Data Catalogue Schema Proposal v05.13

comment.undefined: Alternative identifiers for this indicator from other data publishers or coding schemes (as URIs)

source.value: OAHP Data Catalogue Schema Proposal v05.13

comment.undefined: Provenance is information about entities, activities, and people involved in producing an indicator, which can be used to form assessments about its quality, reliability or trustworthiness. For example a description of the source of this indicator, e.g. academic papers or standards, any calculations or adjustments performed on the raw data and any organisations involved in producing or transforming the data.

source.value: OAHP Data Catalogue Schema Proposal v05.13

comment.undefined: Description of how the indicator might be influenced by other variables or biases, affecting validity.

source.value: OAHP Data Catalogue Schema Proposal v05.13

comment.undefined: The description of one variable or value that is used in the denominator (if this indicator has one) e.g. population size. Multiple data elements may be present as a list.

source.value: OAHP Data Catalogue Schema Proposal v05.13

comment.undefined: An internal prioritisation process status for the dataset or indicator

source.value: OAHP Data Catalogue Schema Proposal v05.13

comment.undefined: The main identifier for the Indicator, e.g. the URI [URL, web link] or other unique identifier in the context of the Catalogue.

source.value: OAHP Data Catalogue Schema Proposal v05.13

comment.undefined: The description of one variable or value that is used in the numerator e.g. number of deaths. Multiple data elements may be present as a list.

source.value: OAHP Data Catalogue Schema Proposal v05.13

comment.undefined: Reasons for Inclusion or Exclusion

source.value: DCAT3

comment.undefined: The legislation that mandates the creation or management of the Dataset.

source.value: PHD

comment.undefined: An internal prioritisation process status for the dataset or indicator

source.value: OAHP Data Catalogue Schema Proposal v05.13

comment.undefined: A list of disaggregation conditions that should be available e.g. male and female

source.value: OAHP Data Catalogue Schema Proposal v05.13

comment.undefined: Any additional informal information about the dataset or indicator

source.value: DCAT3

comment.undefined: Coding systems in use (ex: ICD-10-CM, DGRs, SNOMED=CT, ...)

source.value: OAHP Data Catalogue Schema Proposal v05.13

comment.undefined: Notes on limitations or uncertainty introduced by how the data are collected or calculated.

source.value: OAHP Data Catalogue Schema Proposal v05.13

comment.undefined: A more specific category under the domain (e.g. Population numbers).

source.value: OAHP Data Catalogue Schema Proposal v05.13

comment.undefined: Frequency of indicator update in report, e.g. daily or monthly. This is used with the reporting style to determine the exact frequency and lag of indicator reports.Note that datasets have their own -Frequency of data updates- field to state how often they are updated.

source.value: OAHP Data Catalogue Schema Proposal v05.13

comment.undefined: Technical description of the indicator—what it measures, how it's defined, and any assumptions or limitations in interpretation.

source.value: OAHP Data Catalogue Schema Proposal v05.13

comment.undefined: Guidance on how to understand high or low values—whether they suggest good or poor outcomes.

source.value: OAHP Data Catalogue Schema Proposal v05.13

comment.undefined: The high-level category the indicator falls under (e.g. Demography).

source.value: OAHP Data Catalogue Schema Proposal v05.13

comment.undefined: Identifier, e.g. URL, for the dataset providing a numerator data element. If there are multiple numerator data elements then multiple datasets must be provided as a list.
